The "memory" angle is clinical longitudinal context, not AI memory, cache, bandwidth, or storage behavior.
downgrade_reasons: Narrow medical-imaging application
No direct effect on mainstream AI system design
No concrete memory/storage systems mechanism
Transferability beyond neuroimaging is unclear
out_of_scope.what_would_raise_priority: Evidence that the region-aware longitudinal diffusion approach transfers to broader 3D vision or foundation-model pipelines, or a direct link to AI memory/storage efficiency, would raise priority.
out_of_scope.one_sentence_episode_hook: Can a diffusion model remove evolving brain lesions from longitudinal MRI while preserving 3D anatomy and temporal consistency enough to clean up downstream neuroscience analysis?

out_of_scope.title: Embedded Inter-Subject Variability in Adversarial Learning for Inertial Sensor-Based Human Activity Recognition
out_of_scope.abstract: This paper addresses the problem of Human Activity Recognition (HAR) using data from wearable inertial sensors. An important challenge in HAR is the model's generalization capabilities to new unseen individuals due to inter-subject variability, i.e., the same activity is performed differently by different individuals. To address this problem, we propose a novel deep adversarial framework that integrates the concept of inter-subject variability in the adversarial task, thereby encouraging subject-invariant feature representations and enhancing the classification performance in the HAR problem. Our approach outperforms previous methods in three well-established HAR datasets using a leave-one-subject-out (LOSO) cross-validation. Further results indicate that our proposed adversarial task effectively reduces inter-subject variability among different users in the feature space, and it outperforms adversarial tasks from previous works when integrated into our framework. Code: https://github.com/FranciscoCalatrava/EmbeddedSubjectVariability.git
out_of_scope.why_now: Generalization across unseen users is a real problem for wearable sensing, and the paper shows solid task-level gains with leave-one-subject-out evaluation across multiple HAR datasets. It matters within embedded sensing and activity recognition, but not as a broader AI-systems development this cycle.
out_of_scope.why_not_higher: This is a narrow human activity recognition paper, not something that changes how the wider field builds, trains, serves, or evaluates modern AI systems. The memory/storage connection is absent, and the main contribution is a task-specific adversarial training setup plus benchmark improvement.
downgrade_reasons: narrow application domain
no direct memory or storage mechanism
limited relevance to modern foundation-model practice
benchmark-driven contribution more than field-shaping method
out_of_scope.what_would_raise_priority: Evidence that the subject-invariance method transfers to broader multimodal or on-device AI settings with clear systems or deployment implications would raise it.
out_of_scope.one_sentence_episode_hook: Can adversarial training teach wearables to recognize what you are doing without overfitting to who you are?

out_of_scope.title: Early Warning of Intraoperative Adverse Events via Transformer-Driven Multi-Label Learning
out_of_scope.abstract: Early warning of intraoperative adverse events plays a vital role in reducing surgical risk and improving patient safety. While deep learning has shown promise in predicting the single adverse event, several key challenges remain: overlooking adverse event dependencies, underutilizing heterogeneous clinical data, and suffering from the class imbalance inherent in medical datasets. To address these issues, we construct the first Multi-label Adverse Events dataset (MuAE) for intraoperative adverse events prediction, covering six critical events. Next, we propose a novel Transformerbased multi-label learning framework (IAENet) that combines an improved Time-Aware Feature-wise Linear Modulation (TAFiLM) module for static covariates and dynamic variables robust fusion and complex temporal dependencies modeling. Furthermore, we introduce a Label-Constrained Reweighting Loss (LCRLoss) with co-occurrence regularization to effectively mitigate intra-event imbalance and enforce structured consistency among frequently co-occurring events. Extensive experiments demonstrate that IAENet consistently outperforms strong baselines on 5, 10, and 15-minute early warning tasks, achieving improvements of +5.05%, +2.82%, and +7.57% on average F1 score. These results highlight the potential of IAENet for supporting intelligent intraoperative decision-making in clinical practice.
out_of_scope.why_now: It targets a real high-stakes clinical problem and packages it with a new multi-label intraoperative dataset plus a stronger prediction model. That makes it timely for medical AI, but not especially urgent for the broader AI audience this cycle.
out_of_scope.why_not_higher: This looks like a narrow perioperative prediction paper rather than something that changes mainstream AI modeling, training, serving, or evaluation. The memory/storage connection is effectively absent, and the technical ideas appear mostly domain-bound.
downgrade_reasons: Narrow clinical task paper
No direct memory or storage systems contribution
Limited relevance to how modern AI systems are built or served
Transferability beyond medical event prediction is unclear
out_of_scope.what_would_raise_priority: Evidence that the fusion and label-structure method consistently transfers across multiple non-medical temporal prediction domains or alters real clinical deployment practice would raise it.
out_of_scope.one_sentence_episode_hook: Can a transformer reliably warn surgeons minutes before multiple complications at once, or is this mainly a strong benchmark result for one clinical niche?

out_of_scope.title: Machine Learning for analysis of Multiple Sclerosis cross-tissue bulk and single-cell transcriptomics data
out_of_scope.abstract: Multiple Sclerosis (MS) is a chronic autoimmune disease of the central nervous system whose molecular mechanisms remain incompletely understood. In this study, we developed an end-to-end machine learning pipeline to analyze transcriptomic data from peripheral blood mononuclear cells and cerebrospinal fluid, integrating both bulk microarray and single-cell RNA sequencing datasets (concentrating on CD4+ and B-cells). After rigorous preprocessing, batch correction, and gene declustering, XGBoost classifiers were trained to distinguish MS patients from healthy controls. Explainable AI tools, namely SHapley Additive exPlanations (SHAP), were employed to identify key genes driving classification, and results were compared with Differential Expression Analysis (DEA). SHAP-prioritized genes were further investigated through interaction networks and pathway enrichment analyses. The models achieved strong performance, particularly in CSF B-cells (AUC=0.94) and microarray (AUC=0.86). SHAP gene selection proved to be complementary to classical DEA. Gene clusters identified across multiple datasets highlighted immune activation, non-canonical immune checkpoints (ITK, CLEC2D, KLRG1, CEACAM1), ribosomal and translational programs, ubiquitin-proteasome regulation, lipid trafficking, and Epstein-Barr virus-related pathways. Our integrative and explainable framework reveals complementary insights beyond conventional analysis and provides novel mechanistic hypotheses and potential biomarkers for MS pathogenesis.
out_of_scope.why_now: It is a solid biomedical ML application that uses explainability to generate new MS biomarker and pathway hypotheses across bulk and single-cell datasets. That may matter to computational biology listeners, but it is not timely in a way that would likely pull a broad AI audience right now.
out_of_scope.why_not_higher: The paper does not change how modern AI systems are built, trained, served, or evaluated, and the memory/storage link is absent. Its methods are mostly established supervised ML and XAI techniques applied to a narrow disease setting.
downgrade_reasons: narrow biomedical application
no direct AI systems impact
no meaningful memory/storage contribution
limited transfer beyond transcriptomics classification
out_of_scope.what_would_raise_priority: A clearer transferable method for multimodal biological foundation models or a concrete systems result that changes large-scale training or inference would raise priority.
out_of_scope.one_sentence_episode_hook: Can explainable ML on blood and cerebrospinal fluid data uncover hidden immune signatures of multiple sclerosis, even if the AI methods themselves are familiar?
